title: Introduction to Computing
summary: The gentlest introduction to programming at UofA. Designed for students with zero background.

title: Introduction to the Foundations of Computation I
summary: First-year Python course. Variables, control flow, functions, recursion, basic data structures.

title: Introduction to the Foundations of Computation II
summary: Continues from 174. Object-oriented programming, more complex data structures, recursion deepened. The jump from 174 to 175 catches a lot of students by surprise.

title: Introduction to Data Science
summary: Introduction to data acquisition, basic data manipulation (cleaning, outlier detection), analysis (regression, clustering, classification), basic statistics and machine learning tools, information visualization to communicate information fr

title: Introduction to Principles and Techniques of Data Science
summary: This course introduces data science to students with prior computing experience.

title: Ethics of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
summary: This course focuses on ethics issues in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science (DS).

title: Practical Programming Methodology
summary: The rite of passage for any UofA programmer, no matter the experience.

title: Algorithms I
summary: Big-O analysis, sorting, graphs, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms. This is THE interview course. Everything you will be asked in a technical interview draws from this material.

title: Introduction to Digital Image Processing
summary: An introduction to basic digital image processing theory, and the tools that make advanced image manipulation possible for ordinary users.

title: Computer Organization and Architecture I
summary: Assembly language, MIPS, CPU design, memory hierarchy. Consistently cited by students as the hardest course in the program.

title: Computers and Games
summary: An interdisciplinary course for students in Science, Arts, and other faculties.

title: Game Artificial Intelligence
summary: The purpose of this course is for students to gain a breadth of understanding of the AI approaches employed in digital games.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
summary: Introduction to artificial intelligence focusing on techniques for building intelligent software systems and agents.

title: Machine Learning I
summary: Statistics-heavy intro to ML: linear algebra, probability, gradient descent, regression, classification. This is not a "here's how to use scikit-learn" course. It's mathematical foundations.

title: Formal Systems and Logic in Computing Science
summary: Propositional logic, predicate logic, proofs, set theory. Dry but necessary. Many students find it tedious; the students who actually absorb the proof-writing skills are better prepared for theory courses.

title: Introduction to Tangible Computing I
summary: The alternative to 174/175. Uses Raspberry Pi hardware, covers Python deeply in 274, then C++ in 275.

title: Introduction to Tangible Computing II
summary: The alternative to 174/175. Uses Raspberry Pi hardware, covers Python deeply in 274, then C++ in 275.

title: Introduction to File and Database Management
summary: SQL, relational models, NoSQL (MongoDB), query optimization basics. Two big assignments and two group projects in most sections (a Python SQLite project and a MongoDB project).

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for new course offerings that may be offered in a given term.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for new course offerings that may be offered in a given term.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for new course offerings that may be offered in a given term.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for a one on one individual study course between a student and an instructor.

title: Computers and Society
summary: Social, ethical, professional, economic, and legal issues in the development and deployment of computer technology in society.

title: Introduction to Software Engineering
summary: Android development project + software engineering concepts (design patterns, UML, requirements). The project is the course. Your experience with 301 is almost entirely determined by your team.

title: Introduction to Human Computer Interaction
summary: A complementary course to introductory software engineering focused on a user-centered approach to software design.

title: Algorithmics in Practice
summary: This course is focused on algorithmic problems, where a solution involves properly understanding a written description, designing an efficient algorithm to solve the problem, and then correctly implementing the solution.

title: Algorithms II
summary: The second course of a two-course sequence on algorithm design.

title: 3D Modeling and Animation
summary: An introductory course on the theory and applications of computer based 3D modeling and animation.

title: Video Processing and Applications
summary: An introductory course in video data processing, with implementations in programming languages, like Python, C and MATLAB, using libraries like OpenCV.

title: Introduction to Robotics and Mechatronics
summary: Algorithms and software paradigms for robot programming; mathematical modeling of robot arms and rovers including kinematics, and an introduction to dynamics and control; sensors, motors and their modeling; basics of image processing and ma

title: Computer Networks
summary: TCP/IP, routing, DNS, the OSI model, socket programming. Often cited as "boring but useful." Students who go on to do backend or infrastructure work often say they wished they'd paid more attention.

title: Non-Procedural Programming Languages
summary: Functional programming (Lisp/Scheme or Haskell depending on the semester), logic programming. Teaches a completely different way of thinking about computation.

title: Visual Recognition
summary: Introduction to visual recognition to recognize objects and classify scenes or images automatically by a computer.

title: Computer Organization and Architecture II
summary: CMOS technology, digital circuits, combinational logic, sequential logic, memory technologies, programmable logic devices, control logic design, register transfer logic, CPU design, hardware description languages.

title: Computational Cryptography
summary: Cryptography is the science of secure communications.

title: Security in a Networked World
summary: Introduction to the concepts and components involved in secure, networked, systems.

title: Introduction to Numerical Methods
summary: Computer arithmetic and errors.

title: Advanced Games Programming
summary: This course focuses on state-of-the-art AI and graphics programming for video games.

title: Games, Puzzles, Algorithms
summary: An introduction to algorithms and theory behind computer programs that solve puzzles (mazes, peg solitaire, etc.) or play games (chess, Go, Hex, etc.).

title: Introduction to Information Retrieval
summary: Introduction to information retrieval focusing on algorithms and data structures for organizing and searching through large collections of documents, and techniques for evaluating the quality of search results.

title: Introduction to Reinforcement Learning
summary: This course provides an introduction to reinforcement learning, which focuses on the study and design of learning agents that interact with a complex, uncertain world to achieve a goal.

title: Search and Planning in Artificial Intelligence
summary: This course provides an introduction to search and planning in artificial intelligence.

title: Operating System Concepts
summary: Processes, threads, scheduling, memory management, file systems, IPC. One of the most prof-dependent courses in the program; the same material can be a very different experience depending on who's teaching.

title: Introduction to GPU Programming
summary: CUDA programming, GPU architecture, parallel algorithms. Not widely discussed on Reddit because not many students take it.

title: Database Management Systems
summary: This course covers the implementation of RDBMSs and some non- relational data models, along with their query languages.

title: Scalable Data-Intensive Analytics
summary: Introduction to scalable computing paradigms suitable for data-intensive analytics, with a focus on abstractions, algorithms, and infrastructure for scaling data science, machine learning, and data engineering tasks using multiple machines.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for new course offerings that may be offered in a given term.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for new course offerings that may be offered in a given term.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for new course offerings that may be offered in a given term.

title: Topics in Computing Science
summary: This topics course is designed for a one on one individual study course between a student and an instructor.

title: Software Process and Product Management
summary: Software development lifecycle, project management, agile, product thinking. More conceptual than 301. Exams are typically manageable; the course leans on readings and projects.

title: Software Quality
summary: Software quality issues are covered.

title: Algorithmics in Competitive Programming
summary: Segment trees, advanced graph algorithms, string algorithms, dynamic programming optimization, computational geometry. The best interview prep course at UofA.

title: Web Applications and Architecture
summary: Web protocols, HTTP, REST APIs, front-end and back-end web development.

title: Introduction to Computer Graphics
summary: 2D and 3D transformation; 3D modeling and viewing; illumination models and shading methods; texture mapping; ray tracing.

title: Experimental Mobile Robotics
summary: A project-based course dealing with the design and implementation of mobile robots to accomplish specific tasks.

title: Introduction to Multimedia Technology
summary: Introduction to basic principles and algorithms used in multimedia systems.

title: Compiler Design
summary: Lexing, parsing, ASTs, semantic analysis, code generation, LLVM. One of the most work-intensive courses in the program.

title: Foundations of Program Analysis
summary: Introduction to the main concepts of program analysis such as intermediate representations, inter-procedural and intra-procedural analysis techniques, call graphs, pointer analysis, and analysis frameworks.

title: Computer Vision
summary: Introduction to the geometry and photometry of the 3D to 2D image formation process for the purpose of computing scene properties from camera images.

title: Computer Systems and Architecture
summary: A discussion of computer system design concepts with stress on modern ideas that have shaped the high-performance architecture of contemporary systems.

title: Search, Knowledge and Simulation
summary: When making decisions in games, computers rely on three main ideas: search, knowledge and simulations.

title: Introduction to Natural Language Processing
summary: Natural language processing (NLP) is a subfield of artificial intelligence concerned with the interactions between computers and human languages.

title: Probabilistic Graphical Models
summary: Probabilistic graphical models (PGMs; including Bayesian Belief Nets, Markov Random Fields, etc.) now contribute significantly to many areas, including expert systems, computer perception (vision and speech), natural language interpretation

title: Machine Learning Essentials
summary: Supervised learning, neural networks, probabilistic models, optimization. The capstone ML course. Quality varies enormously by who is teaching.

title: Machine Learning II
summary: This is the second course of a two-course sequence on machine learning, with a focus on extending to nonlinear modeling with neural networks and higher-dimensional data.

title: Artificial Intelligence Capstone
summary: Students will experience the challenges, and rewards, of working in a team to address a real-world task, related to artificial intelligence or machine learning.

title: Formal Languages, Automata, and Computability
summary: Formal grammars; relationship between grammars and automata; regular expressions; finite state machines; pushdown automata; Turing machines; computability; the halting problem; time and space complexity.

title: Parallel and Distributed Systems
summary: This course provides an undergraduate-level introduction to parallel programming, parallel and distributed systems, and high-performance computing in science and engineering.

title: Honors Seminar
summary: This weekly seminar brings students, researchers, and practitioners together to examine a variety of topics, both foundational and leading edge.

title: Machine Learning Essentials
summary: Learning is essential for many real-world tasks, including recognition, diagnosis, forecasting and data-mining.

title: Machine Learning II
summary: This course expands on machine learning fundamentals with a focus on extending to nonlinear modeling with neural networks and higher-dimensional data.

title: Teaching and Research Methods
summary: This course provides information and resources on teaching and research methods in computing science, and also gives an overview of the research done by faculty in the department.
